chapter_title.en: Description of the Prayer From Its Beginning to Its End

parts.text: 927 - وَ سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ

text: : يَا اِبْنَ عَمِّ خَيْرِ خَلْقِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَا مَعْنَى مَدِّ عُنُقِكَ فِي اَلرُّكُوعِ فَقَالَ «تَأْوِيلُهُ آمَنْتُ بِاللَّهِ وَ لَوْ ضُرِبَتْ عُنُقِي.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.927 - A man asked The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) saying:
"O’ son of the cousin of the best of Allah’s (swt) creation, what is the significance of extending your neck during ruku' (bowing in prayer)?"
Imam Ali (as) responded, "It symbolizes 'I have faith in Allah (swt), even if my neck is struck.'"
